strategy_justification: Playwright browser without proxy successfully loaded full product pages in Cycles 1 and 2, extracting title, asin, rating, review_count, image, description, feature_bullets, availability, url, status_code, and scraped_at correctly. Direct HTTP returns CAPTCHA challenge (opfcaptcha.amazon.com) — confirmed live in product_analysis. Strategy unchanged across 3 cycles.
proxy_justification: Direct connection works with Playwright browser at normal scraping rates (3s+ delay). No proxy escalation needed. Proxy escalation to datacenter then residential only if CAPTCHA is triggered during batch scraping.

url.notes: Canonical /dp/{ASIN} URL format. Validated in Cycles 1 and 2.

src_url.notes: Set from input_urls.json by the scraper.

status_code.notes: Set by the scraper from Playwright response.status.

scraped_at.notes: Set by scraper as ISO-8601 UTC timestamp.

remarks.notes: Set by scraper for CAPTCHA/soft-404/warning detection.

asin.notes: ASIN is 10-char alphanumeric. Extract from URL path first (always reliable), fall back to hidden input.

title.test_result: Apple iPad Air (5th Generation): with M1 chip, 10.9-inch Liquid Retina Display, 64GB, Wi-Fi 6, 12MP front/12MP Back Camera, Touch ID, All-Day Battery Life – Pink
title.notes: Always present on valid product pages. Has leading/trailing whitespace that needs trimming. If missing, page may be invalid/blocked/404.

price.test_result: Correctly empty for OOS products (B09V3KXJPB). Not yet tested on in-stock product across 3 cycles.
price.notes: 6-level fallback chain. CRITICAL: Do NOT use bare .a-price .a-offscreen as primary — it's too broad and will pick up sponsored/similar product prices. Price is absent when product is out of stock. The .a-offscreen span contains the screen-reader-only formatted price string.

original_price.js_extraction: let origEl = document.querySelector('.basisPrice .a-offscreen'); if (!origEl) { origEl = document.querySelector('.a-price.a-text-price .a-offscreen'); } if (!origEl) { origEl = document.querySelector('tr td.a-text-strike .a-offscreen'); } if (origEl) { let val = origEl.textContent.trim(); if (val && !val.includes('/') && val !== product.price) { return val; } } return '';
original_price.notes: 3-level fallback chain. CRITICAL: (1) Only extract original_price when main price was found (OOS guard). (2) Do NOT use bare .a-text-price .a-offscreen — it returns per-unit price. USE .a-price.a-text-price .a-offscreen (double class). (3) Validate the value doesn't contain '/' which indicates per-unit pricing. (4) Only record if value differs from current price.

availability.logic: Check element presence, not text content
availability.js_extraction: const outOfStock = !!document.getElementById('outOfStock'); const atc = !!document.getElementById('add-to-cart-button'); const buyNow = !!document.getElementById('buy-now-button'); const availDiv = document.getElementById('availability'); let text = availDiv ? availDiv.textContent.toLowerCase() : ''; if (outOfStock) return 'Out of Stock'; if (text.includes('currently unavailable')) return 'Out of Stock'; if (text.includes('only') && text.includes('left')) return 'Low Stock'; if (atc || buyNow) return 'In Stock'; return 'Unknown';
availability.test_result: Out of Stock (B09V3KXJPB) — CORRECT. Unknown (B07VGRJDFY in rate-limited run) — acceptable fallback.
availability.notes: CRITICAL: Do NOT parse #availability text directly — it contains embedded JSON for in-stock items. Use element presence checks. Valid outputs: 'In Stock', 'Out of Stock', 'Low Stock', 'Unknown'.

image.notes: Main product image src attribute. Validated in both cycles.

rating.notes: Format: 'X.Y out of 5 stars'. Only present if product has reviews.

review_count.notes: Format varies: 'X,XXX ratings' or '(X,XXX)'. Only present if product has reviews.

brand.css_fallback: table.prodDetTable tr (label lookup: 'Brand', 'Brand Name', 'Manufacturer'), #brand, a.bylineInfo, a[href*='/brand/']
brand.js_extraction: const byline = document.getElementById('bylineInfo'); if (byline) { return byline.textContent.replace('Brand: ', '').replace('Visit the ', '').replace(' Store', '').trim(); } const table = document.querySelector('table.prodDetTable'); if (table) { for (const row of table.querySelectorAll('tr')) { const th = row.querySelector('th'); const td = row.querySelector('td'); if (th && td && (th.textContent.trim() === 'Brand' || th.textContent.trim() === 'Brand Name' || th.textContent.trim() === 'Manufacturer')) { return td.textContent.trim().split(',')[0].trim(); } } } return '';
brand.test_result: Apple (B09V3KXJPB) — CORRECT. Empty (B07VGRJDFY Nintendo Switch) — LOW severity failure.
brand.notes: PRIMARY: bylineInfo link text. FALLBACK: product details table with label lookup. CRITICAL FIX FOR CYCLE 3: The current code checks a.bylineInfo as a CSS class but Nintendo Switch page may use a different element. Add a broader fallback: document.querySelector('.a-row .a-link-normal[href*="store"]') which matches the 'Visit the Nintendo Store' link pattern. Also add: document.querySelector('a#bylineInfo, a.bylineInfo, #bylineInfo') — checking both ID and class variants in the primary selector.

breadcrumbs.css_fallback: #wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container ul li a, .a-breadcrumb a, nav.a-breadcrumb a
breadcrumbs.test_result: Empty array in both Cycles 1 and 2 (pre-fix). Fix implemented in Cycle 3 but NOT validated due to CDP crash bug.
breadcrumbs.retry_3_notes: The scraper code correctly has page.wait_for_selector('#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container a', timeout=5000) added before extraction (from Cycle 2 feedback). However the scraper crashed on get_browser() before reaching extraction. The fix logic is sound — breadcrumbs render lazily via JavaScript and need an explicit wait after productTitle loads. If still empty after the wait, consider: (1) The container may exist but links may be in a shadow DOM or iframe — check document.querySelector('#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container').innerHTML, (2) Try waiting for the container first then querying children, (3) The selector may need to include nested list items: '#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container ul a'.
breadcrumbs.notes: CRITICAL FIX NEEDED: The selector #wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container a is correct for Amazon, but needs explicit wait. Code fix from Cycle 2 is correctly implemented but unvalidated. This is the ONLY field not yet validated in a successful live run.

description.test_result: Full multi-paragraph description for B07VGRJDFY. Empty for B09V3KXJPB (OOS — description section may not render).
description.notes: Two sources: #productDescription (full description) and #feature-bullets (bullet points). Use #productDescription as primary.

feature_bullets.test_result: [9 bullet items for B09V3KXJPB, 5 bullet items for B07VGRJDFY]
feature_bullets.notes: Key product features as bullet points. Usually 5-8 items.

sku.notes: SKU on Amazon is typically the ASIN. Use ASIN value directly.

checks: #productTitle element is missing (indicates block or invalid page)
captcha_detection.action: If CAPTCHA detected: wait 5-10 seconds, retry. If persistent, escalate proxy tier: none -> datacenter -> residential.

extraction_notes: Extract availability BEFORE price — out-of-stock products have no price elements.
Only extract original_price when price was found (OOS guard).
Do NOT use bare .a-price .a-offscreen for price — too broad.
Do NOT use bare .a-text-price .a-offscreen for original_price — returns per-unit price. USE .a-price.a-text-price .a-offscreen.
Do NOT parse #availability text directly — contains embedded JSON. Use element presence checks.
Amazon 404s return HTTP 200 with no #productTitle. Detect by checking title element existence.
URLs may contain many tracking params. Clean to /dp/{ASIN} format before storing.
original_price validation: skip value if it contains '/' (per-unit price) or equals current price.
BREADCRUMBS: Add page.wait_for_selector('#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container a', timeout=5000) BEFORE extraction. Breadcrumbs render lazily and need explicit wait.
Breadcrumbs should be extracted BEFORE price (moved up in priority order) since they are a non-price field that needs the explicit wait to succeed.

rate_limiting.justification: Amazon has IP-based rate limiting. Too many rapid requests trigger CAPTCHA. 3s delay with 0-1s jitter is safe for sustained scraping.

critical_fix.problem: get_browser() crashes with unhandled exception when BROWSER_CDP_ENDPOINT env var is set but CDP endpoint is unreachable. The connect_over_cdp() call raises 'socket hang up' with no try/except fallback.
critical_fix.fix: Wrap connect_over_cdp() in try/except with fallback to local pw.chromium.launch(). This is the ONLY blocking issue — once fixed, the scraper should work as it did in Cycles 1 and 2.

medium_fix.problem: Breadcrumb fix correctly implemented in code (page.wait_for_selector '#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container a' timeout=5000) but not validated because CDP crash prevented any live scraping.
medium_fix.action: No code change needed. The fix is correct. After CDP crash fix, re-run --sample to validate breadcrumbs.

low_fix.problem: Brand empty for B07VGRJDFY (Nintendo Switch). Current 4-level fallback fails because #bylineInfo ID not found and table.prodDetTable/#brand/a.bylineInfo also not found.
low_fix.fix: Add broader byline fallback selectors: document.querySelector('.a-row .a-link-normal[href*="store"]') to catch 'Visit the Nintendo Store' link pattern. Also change primary selector from a#bylineInfo to check both ID and class: document.querySelector('a#bylineInfo, a.bylineInfo, #bylineInfo').
low_fix.action: Code writer should add 5th fallback in brand extraction JS: const storeLink = document.querySelector('.a-row .a-link-normal[href*="store"]'); if (storeLink) { const text = storeLink.textContent.trim(); return text.replace('Visit the ', '').replace(' Store', '').trim(); }

escalation_note: Cycle 3 — no strategy or proxy change needed. The strategy (playwright, proxy_tier none) is correct and verified in Cycles 1-2. The only blocking issue is the CDP connection crash bug in get_browser(), which is a code-level fix, not a strategy fix. After the CDP fallback fix is applied, confidence should return to 0.85+.
confidence_notes: Confidence 0.80 (up from 0.75 in test report): Strategy (playwright, none proxy) is verified working in Cycles 1-2. All core fields validated. CDP crash is a code bug not a strategy issue — fix is straightforward. Breadcrumbs fix is correctly coded but unvalidated (medium severity). Brand has minor edge case (low severity). Price/original_price untested on in-stock/sale products but selectors are comprehensive. Once CDP crash is fixed and breadcrumbs validate, confidence should be 0.90+.
